title: Chunlei Liu | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: Chunlei Liu | EECS at UC Berkeley Chunlei Liu: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo Chunlei Liu Professor Contact Information 574 Cory Hall tel: (510)664 7596 chunlei.liu@eecs.berkeley.edu Office Hours Monday 1-2pm or by appointment, 574 Cory Links Personal Homepage Publications Dissertations Research Support Farah Pranawahadi 631 Davis farah@berkeley.edu Biography Chunlei Liu is a Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences, and the Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute at the University of California, Berkeley. He received a B.S. in Physics from Peking University, a M.S. in Physics from the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill and a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University. Before joining UC Berkeley, he was an Associate Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Engineering at Duke University. His current research interests are brain imaging and modulation. He develops MRI and signal processing techniques to study brain structure and function. His group also works on technologies for remote modulation of neuronal activities. He received NIH Pathway to Independence Award (K99) in 2006, ISMRM Young Investigator Award finalist in 2007 and RSNA Margulis Award in 2015. In 2021, he was elected Fellow of ISMRM and Fellow of AIMBE (American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering). He is a Weill Neurohub Investigator. Education 2005, Ph.D., Electrical Engineering, Stanford University 2003, M.S., Management Science and Engineering, Stanford University 2000, M.S., Physics, University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill 1998, B.S., Physics, Peking University, Beijing Research Areas Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO) Signal Processing (SP) Brain Imaging; Magnetic Resonance Imaging; Remote Neural Modulation Research Centers Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute (HWNI) Teaching Schedule EE 375.
